schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Angular Harmonic Spectrum (Detects the 7 Lines Objectively): I(r,φ)

theory.canonical_statement: ### A — Angular harmonic spectrum (detects the “7 lines” objectively) - Transform image to polar coordinates around the bright core: \(I(r,\phi)\). - Integrate radially over a ring outside core: \(A(\phi)=\int_{r_0}^{r_1} I(r,\phi)\,dr.\) - Do an angular FFT of \(A(\phi)\): \(A_m = \int A(\phi) e^{-im\phi} d\phi\). - **Interpretation:** large |A_m| at m≈7 means a real 7-fold angular symmetry. If peaks at m and at -m, that explains paired opposite spokes. - **Verdict:** number & phase of top peaks → actual count and orientation.
theory.technical_interpretation: Angular Harmonic Spectrum (Detects the 7 Lines Objectively): I(r,φ) is a symbolic expression, written I(r,φ). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from φ, I, r. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where transform image to polar coordinates around the bright core
